value.Byline: Brandon Mulder and Chris Essig The Texas Tribune
value.Title: Gas power makes big comeback in Texas Gas power leapfrogs wind for first time in 10 years in Texas' grid connection queue

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ6-2T83-RYG7-92XM-00000-00Gas power makes big comeback in Texas2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:48ZLexisNexisAPg. 7AGas power makes big comeback in TexasGas power leapfrogs wind for first time in 10 years in Texas' grid connection queueBrandon Mulder and Chris Essig The Texas Tribune

A decade ago, wind power was surging in popularity and attracting huge investments that made Texas a national leader in renewable energy. But today, gas generation is making a big comeback, driven by a wave of data centers flooding into the state.

For the last six months, the volume of gas generation in the Texas grid's interconnection queue - the yearslong waiting list for electric generators wanting to connect to the grid - has surpassed wind. It's the first time since January 2016 that gas has overtaken wind in the queue, a shift that reflects the policy and economic headwinds facing the wind industry and data centers favoring gas power as they seek to cash in on the artificial intelligence boom.

"The data center explosion and their desire for 24/7 power probably excited a lot of gas developers, and that gas queue got bigger," said University of Texas professor of energy regulation David Spence.

Like every power grid operator in the U.S., the Electric Reliability Council of Texas, or ERCOT, uses the interconnection queue to manage and plan for new power generation coming online. But not all projects in the queue ultimately reach completion. According to the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, or LBNL, only 22% of projects in ERCOT's queue actually get built, which is still the highest percentage of any grid in the nation.

Still, the queue gives an early indication of how the grid is projected to evolve in the future. Solar and battery projects dominate, accounting for 75% of the 458,000 megawatts in the queue, with gas and wind projects making up the rest.

But over the last three years, the volume of gas projects in the queue has jumped by more than 400%, from 12,500 megawatts in March 2023 to nearly 64,000 megawatts as of last month, according to ERCOT data. Meanwhile, wind projects have grown at a much slower clip, increasing by 87% from 25,700 to 48,000 megawatts over the same period.

The gas queue is getting a boost from the Texas Energy Fund, a program passed by the Legislature in 2023 that provides low-interest loans to developers of so-called dispatchable power that can easily be ramped up or down according to grid demand, such as gas-fired power plants.

Centex facility

The $10 billion taxpayer-backed fund officially produced its first natural gas power plant this week, the Houston Chronicle reported. The Pin Oak Creek facility was announced in 2023 by Houston-based Calpine, a company that has since been acquired by Baltimore-based Constellation. It is located in Freestone County, about 75 miles east of Waco in Central Texas, and adds 460 megawatts of electricity generation, enough to power approximately 115,000 homes during the hottest summer hours.

About 9,000 megawatts worth of projects in the queue are getting loans from the Energy Fund, a large portion of which are "near-term gas projects we expect to come online," an ERCOT spokesperson said.

But the strongest driving force behind the gas ramp-up is the long list of companies looking to build data centers in Texas.

ERCOT data shows around 360,000 megawatts of power demand tied to prospective data center projects - a volume that would by itself more than quadruple the grid's record peak demand of 85,500 megawatts set in August 2023.

If anything close to that number actually gets built, ERCOT CEO Pablo Vegas told a Senate committee earlier this month, "then we're going to clearly need a lot more of [gas] generation in order to have a balanced and reliable grid."

ERCOT's gas embrace

Vegas sees the growth of gas projects on the queue as a welcome trend. For too long, he said, ERCOT's energy market hasn't been attracting dispatchable energy generation like gas plants.

It's a symptom of how the market was designed 25 years ago, when the Texas Legislature passed sweeping reforms that deregulated the market. Restructuring in 1999 created a competitive wholesale and retail power market and introduced a renewable energy requirement.

That incentivized bringing cheap electricity to the grid, Vegas said, and it kick-started renewable energy development that boomed through the late 2000s and early 2010s.

But Vegas said the new market design failed to value the different strengths of power sources, and those differences matter for grid reliability.

Renewables provide cheap, clean electricity but are only intermittently available when the sun is shining and wind is blowing. Battery power - which pulls electricity from the grid during off-peak hours then sends it back to the grid during peak hours - can be dispatched when needed, but it has duration limitations.

Thermal generators like gas and coal don't have similar limits as long as the fuel is available to run them.

"We've seen this explosion of wind and solar, and now batteries, to the complete exclusion of growth in the natural gas system, because economically we're not valuing the characteristics of gas generation that is so important for long-term reliability," Vegas said. "We need to change that somewhere in the market design to recognize the reliability characteristics of the generating source.

Data centers

But bringing gas-fired power onto the grid faces its own hurdles. For the last several years, the supply chain for gas turbines - the engines that power plants use to generate electricity - has seen a major bottleneck caused in large part by surging demand from data centers outstripping manufacturing capacity. According to Wood Mackenzie, an energy research and consulting firm, orders for a turbine today may take until 2031 to arrive.

However, data centers and gas power developers are finding creative ways around this problem, including repurposing turbines originally designed for aircraft or cruise ships, according to a recent report from the energy data company Cleanview.

In El Paso, for instance, the city's electric utility is planning to build a gas plant using a novel design that will tie together 813 small gas generators, which are typically used as backup power units at hospitals or manufacturing facilities, to produce 366 megawatts for a data center being built by Meta. And in Mississippi, Elon Musk's AI company xAI purchased and revived a dormant power plant last year to power a data center that helps train xAI's chatbot Grok.

"The perceived economic loss of not getting your data center up and running in a year is valued in the billions of dollars," said University of Texas grid researcher Joshua Rhodes. "That will drive people to very out-of-the-box solutions for these types of things."

Wind's slowdown

On the other side of the equation is wind's shrinking market share. Since its peak in 2018, when wind composed around 50% of the megawatts in the interconnection queue, wind has now fallen behind every other major generation source.

The headwinds facing wind today are multifold, experts say, and extend beyond the recent cuts to tax credits for renewable projects.

Wind's primary challenge stems from its earlier success. After two decades of immense growth across the state, all of the prime development locations have been taken. Parcels across West Texas and the coastal region - where winds are strong, land is accessible, and transmission lines are available to send the electricity to the grid - have mostly been developed already.

"Texas has a lot of land, but the low-hanging fruit has been picked over by all the wind development that has happened there," said Joseph Rand, an energy markets researcher at LBNL.

And because the West Texas and coastal regions have become saturated, the existing transmission lines have grown congested, forcing generators to curtail their power output - which cuts into their profits.

To solve that issue, ERCOT is advancing plans to update its transmission system again, which will include three major 765 kilovolt lines capable of carrying more electricity than any existing line in Texas.

"If I were a wind developer, I might not want to build today and face that curtailment risk for the first few years of my project's lifespan," Rand said.

Wind's challenges also stem from solar's soaring success in Texas. While technology costs for wind and solar have both fallen over the last two decades, the cost of solar energy has fallen more rapidly, from around $160 per megawatt-hour in 2010 to around $70 in 2024, LBNL data shows.

"Because the cost of building a solar farm has gone down so dramatically over the last five to six years, it just becomes a better business decision to build solar and, increasingly, solar plus [battery] storage," said Judd Messer, Texas vice president of the clean energy trade association Advanced Power Alliance.

Lastly, the wind industry faces policy uncertainty that has made it difficult for wind projects to attract financing. Trump administration policies that have slowed or paused federal approvals necessary for wind projects to reach completion have caused investors to shy away from the industry.

In Texas, which created a grid designed to avoid federal regulation by operating almost entirely within state lines, the federal government can still hamper renewables via the Federal Aviation Administration's 200-foot rule, which requires construction permits for all structures over 200 feet tall, such as onshore wind turbines.

Last summer, for instance, the U.S. Department of Transportation announced that the FAA "will thoroughly evaluate proposed wind turbines to ensure they do not pose a danger to aviation," indicating that the routine permit may become a hurdle for onshore wind.

"This kind of arbitrary executive-level uncertainty is really spooking people," said Messer.

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-KD23-RXGW-R28P-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS WITH PEER COMPARISONS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-2TV3-S0S7-12TM-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:40ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-436X-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT SECTOR NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: How PSEG Renewable Transmission Could Address Right-of-Entry Challenges Following PSEG v. Arentz Family Eminent Domain Access Dispute in Energy Transmission Sector *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-KSM3-S0XP-40M1-00000-00How PSEG Renewable Transmission Could Address Right-of-Entry Challenges Following PSEG v. Arentz Family Eminent Domain Access Dispute in Energy Transmission Sector *Updated*2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisHow PSEG Renewable Transmission Could Address Right-of-Entry Challenges Following PSEG v. Arentz Family Eminent Domain Access Dispute in Energy Transmission Sector *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

Legal actions pursuing temporary property access for surveys related to the proposed Maryland Piedmont Reliability Project may influence infrastructure development methods within the energy transmission sector. Stakeholders should observe how court rulings concerning preliminary injunctions and property rights affect timelines for necessary reliability projects, which may impact supply chain stability and investment decisions. The outcomes of these proceedings could establish precedents for balancing public utility requirements against private landowner interests, resulting in increased regulatory review of permitting processes. This scenario highlights broader competitive dynamics, wherein effective management may facilitate project progression while delays provide opportunities for industry competitors. Market participants should review compliance strategies to minimize risks associated with comparable disputes.

Case Background

PSEG Renewable Transmission LLC initiated legal proceedings against Arentz Family, LP and over 100 other landowners in the U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland (1:2025cv01235) on or around April 15, 2025. The filings request temporary right-of-entry to conduct field surveys and environmental data collection required for the Maryland Piedmont Reliability Project (MPRP), an approximately 67-mile 500 kV transmission line spanning Baltimore, Carroll, and Frederick counties. The project, contracted through PJM Interconnection to support grid reliability, necessitates thorough surveys to complete the Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity (CPCN) application for the Maryland Public Service Commission. Related companion cases include 1:2025cv02296 (Alvi Properties) and 1:25-cv-03352 (Belfast Farms). Landowners have contested the access requests, stating the actions represent an early exercise of eminent domain prior to securing full regulatory approvals.

Legal Proceedings

The proceedings progressed promptly to hearings regarding preliminary injunctions. On June 20, 2025, Judge Adam B. Abelson issued a memorandum opinion granting PSEG access to conduct the surveys. Subsequent motions addressed clarifications regarding hunting restrictions during the survey periods and procedures for future assistance. Discovery and related filings remain active across the consolidated matters, with appeals registered to the Fourth Circuit. Public sentiment, as indicated by local reports and landowner organizations, demonstrates opposition focused on property rights, whereas PSEG notes the necessity of the project for grid reliability in the context of increasing energy demand. Projected timelines for the complete CPCN review and potential condemnation phases are ongoing, with estimates suggesting additional months before resolution.

Legal Outcomes and Policy Implications

Potential outcomes involve the affirmation of access orders, the establishment of settlements regarding survey protocols, or appellate reversals that may postpone the project. Favorable decisions for PSEG could assist in the progression of the CPCN, whereas adverse rulings might require redesigns or alternative routing plans. This matter intersects with developing standards regarding utility eminent domain and environmental reviews under state and federal frameworks, which may affect comparable infrastructure initiatives nationally. Broader implications include ESG considerations related to community engagement and the acceleration of infrastructure necessary for renewable energy integration.

Why This Case Is Challenging for PSEG Renewable Transmission

Legal Liabilities

Exposure to extended litigation and appeals that may prolong timelines and increase legal expenditures estimated in the millions.

Risk of court-imposed constraints on survey methodologies or schedules, complicating the collection of data for regulatory submissions.

Financial Exposure

Potential project delays that may increase overall development expenses by tens of millions due to extended carrying costs and redesign requirements.

Uncertainty regarding the acquisition of financing or partnerships during the ongoing disputes.

Reputational Damage

Community and landowner opposition may reduce public support for future initiatives in the region.

Media coverage focusing on the opposition may influence stakeholder views regarding corporate responsibility.

Operational Disruptions

An inability to gather timely field data presents risks of incomplete CPCN submissions and subsequent project suspensions.

Coordination difficulties involving survey teams operating across contested properties.

Regulatory Scrutiny

Increased PSC and environmental review attention resulting from the disputes, potentially resulting in more stringent conditions.

Precedent-setting impacts regarding eminent domain authority for utilities prior to CPCN approval.

Market Position

Project delays may permit competitors to progress with alternative transmission solutions, affecting PSEG's position within PJM markets.

Difficulties in sustaining project momentum may impact regional reliability contributions and the performance of existing contracts.

Overall Impact on the Industry

This dispute illustrates the tensions present in energy transmission development, where accelerated infrastructure requirements intersect with property rights. The situation may result in increased compliance costs, promote collaborative routing methodologies, and influence the development of non-invasive surveying technologies. Across the sector, it may encourage utilities to allocate more resources toward stakeholder engagement and alternative project designs to minimize legal risks.

Navigating a Dual-Edged Sword

PSEG may utilize court-granted access to proceed with necessary surveys while executing community outreach and transparent communication to manage concerns. Effective administration may reinforce its position in executing reliability projects, providing opportunities to demonstrate responsible development procedures. However, active appeals and opposition necessitate strategic resource allocation to balance immediate legal defenses with long-term operational objectives within a regulated environment.

Activities

Public Service Enterprise Group Inc. (PSEG), a leading energy holding company headquartered in New Jersey, USA, primarily operates through its subsidiaries to deliver reliable electricity and natural gas services across the northeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass electric generation, transmission, and distribution, as well as natural gas utility operations, serving millions of customers with a focus on safety, efficiency, and infrastructure modernization. PSEG has strategically expanded into renewable energy sources, with solar and wind power emerging as the fastest-growing segments, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory policies aimed at reducing carbon emissions. This diversification not only enhances the company's sustainability profile but also positions it for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market, while maintaining a strong commitment to operational excellence and community engagement. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Public Service Enterprise Group Inc. (NYSE:PEG) is a leading energy company advancing through strategic investments in PSEG AI infrastructure. At the forefront of innovation, Public Service Enterprise Group AI is fueling growth, making PSEG AI stock (NYSE:PEG) a key consideration in PSEG stock analysis. Investing in PSEG AI transformation promises enhancements in PSEG smart grid solutions, and PSEG grid modernization projects. The integration of PSEG energy analytics platform and PSEG grid AI platform supports PSEG predictive maintenance, with PSEG predictive outage detection minimizing disruptions. Advanced PSEG load forecasting and PSEG demand response services optimize operations. PSEG renewable energy projects, including PSEG solar projects and PSEG wind energy, complement PSEG battery storage solutions, enhancing PSEG energy storage optimization. Additionally, PSEG EV charging network expansion and PSEG distributed energy resources management emphasize sustainability. PSEG microgrid solutions and PSEG smart meters deployment, along with PSEQ digital substations, fortify PSEG transmission and distribution. PSEG nuclear generation, a part of PSEG power generation fleet, and PSEG energy-as-a-service leverage PSEG data centers and edge computing. PSEG utility AI solutions and PSEG infrastructure investments drive growth.</

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [4 Press Releases]

+ WRI & ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION: EARLY CLIMATE HEALTH INVESTMENTS GENERATE 68-FOLD GAINS IN LOW- AND MIDDLE-INCOME COUNTRIES

New World Resources Institute analysis, supported by The Rockefeller Foundation, reveals that every $1 invested in preparing for climate-caused health risks can yield up to $68 in benefits for communities in Africa, Asia, Latin America and the Caribbean, and the Middle East

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION DENGUEAI: NEW MODEL THAT ANTICIPATES OUTBREAKS WITH 93% EFFECTIVENESS AND THREE WEEKS ADVANCE NOTICE

Cali, Colombia | May 4, 2026 - The City of Santiago de Cali, Universidad Icesi, and The Rockefeller Foundation today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) aimed at reducing the incidence of dengue in the city and strengthening preparedness and response capacities for future epidemics.

Source : company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHM-3HK3-RX9M-0119-00000-00American Electric Power May Assess Competitive Edges Following PSEG v. Alvi Properties Access Challenge in Utility Infrastructure Sector *Updated*2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisAmerican Electric Power May Assess Competitive Edges Following PSEG v. Alvi Properties Access Challenge in Utility Infrastructure Sector *Updated*

NYSE STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

The legal proceedings requesting temporary property access for surveys related to the proposed Maryland Piedmont Reliability Project could influence infrastructure development approaches within the energy transmission industry. Stakeholders should observe how judicial decisions regarding preliminary injunctions and property rights might impact the schedules for necessary reliability projects, which could subsequently affect supply chain consistency and investment planning. The results may establish precedents for weighing public utility requirements against private landowner rights, resulting in increased regulatory review of the permitting process. This scenario highlights general competitive factors, where efficient management may facilitate project progression, whereas operational delays could provide operational opportunities for industry competitors. Market participants should review their compliance frameworks to minimize risks associated with similar property access disputes.

Background of the Case

The litigation, which commenced on July 15, 2025, centers on PSEG Renewable Transmission LLC requesting a court-mandated temporary right-of-entry in the U.S. District Court for the District of Maryland (1:2025cv02296) to conduct surveys on parcels along the Maryland Piedmont Reliability Project corridor. This approximately 67-mile, 500 kV transmission line is intended to facilitate PJM grid reliability. Property owners contested the access requests, resulting in preliminary injunctions issued in September 2025 with subsequent judicial clarifications. The proceeding aligns with the initial PSEG filing and focuses on preliminary data collection pursuant to Maryland real property statutes. There are no primary damages claims involved; the proceedings are strictly focused on property access rights.

Competitive Impacts on American Electric Power (AEP)

American Electric Power's transmission portfolio may benefit from potential delays in the MPRP, allowing the company to concentrate on internal initiatives within reliability markets. Ongoing public discourse might influence AEP to adjust its operational strategies.

Possible Opportunities for American Electric Power (AEP)

Apply established eminent domain experience in future bidding processes.

Formulate joint infrastructure projects in regions with high demand.

Communicate grid benefits through targeted stakeholder engagement.

Increase operations within overlapping PJM transmission segments.

Why This Case Is Challenging for American Electric Power (AEP)

(Parallel indirect spillover analysis regarding operational standards, associated costs, and project timelines.)

Key Challenges for American Electric Power (AEP)

Maintaining cross-jurisdictional regulatory compliance.

Managing increased costs associated with stakeholder opposition.

Adapting to extended project development schedules.

Incorporating ESG factors during active disputes.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

Increase allocation of specialized legal resources.

Deploy digital platforms for stakeholder communication.

Participate in the development of industry best practices.

Advance the timeline for integrated renewable energy projects.

Key Measures: Division-wide alignment facilitates efficient risk management through defined tracking protocols.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Revenue generation resulting from schedule optimizations.

Market differentiation achieved through reliable project execution.

Improvement of stakeholder engagement methodologies.

Expansion of collaborative industry networks.

Potential Losses

Escalation of operational costs.

Adjustments to project timelines.

Increased market competition.

Impacts on broader corporate perception.

Final Thoughts

American Electric Power has the capacity to leverage its scale and expertise in response to the PSEG Alvi matter. A focus on adaptive execution and strategic alignment facilitates the management of operational challenges while supporting sustained leadership in transmission infrastructure development.

Activities

Public Service Enterprise Group Inc. (PSEG), a leading energy holding company headquartered in New Jersey, USA, primarily operates through its subsidiaries to deliver reliable electricity and natural gas services across the northeastern United States. Its core business activities encompass electric generation, transmission, and distribution, as well as natural gas utility operations, serving millions of customers with a focus on safety, efficiency, and infrastructure modernization. PSEG has strategically expanded into renewable energy sources, with solar and wind power emerging as the fastest-growing segments, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory policies aimed at reducing carbon emissions. This diversification not only enhances the company's sustainability profile but also positions it for long-term growth in a transitioning energy market, while maintaining a strong commitment to operational excellence and community engagement. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Public Service Enterprise Group Inc. (NYSE:PEG) is a leading energy company advancing through strategic investments in PSEG AI infrastructure. At the forefront of innovation, Public Service Enterprise Group AI is fueling growth, making PSEG AI stock (NYSE:PEG) a key consideration in PSEG stock analysis. Investing in PSEG AI transformation promises enhancements in PSEG smart grid solutions, and PSEG grid modernization projects. The integration of PSEG energy analytics platform and PSEG grid AI platform supports PSEG predictive maintenance, with PSEG predictive outage detection minimizing disruptions. Advanced PSEG load forecasting and PSEG demand response services optimize operations. PSEG renewable energy projects, including PSEG solar projects and PSEG wind energy, complement PSEG battery storage solutions, enhancing PSEG energy storage optimization. Additionally, PSEG EV charging network expansion and PSEG distributed energy resources management emphasize sustainability. PSEG microgrid solutions and PSEG smart meters deployment, along with PSEQ digital substations, fortify PSEG transmission and distribution. PSEG nuclear generation, a part of PSEG power generation fleet, and PSEG energy-as-a-service leverage PSEG data centers and edge computing. PSEG utility AI solutions and PSEG infrastructure investments drive growth.</
