urn:contentItem:6JHJ-4M53-RXNT-64N0-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:40ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-YCV3-SHN4-P3D5-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - INDUSTRY WATCH: SECTOR TRENDS AND PEER PERSPECTIVES

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Balcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHV-XTM3-RRVT-34P0-00000-00Balcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:14ZLexisNexisBalcony solar bill dies in Illinois after union voices opposition

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Kari Lydersen

May 8th, 2026 ( Canary Media - Delivered by Newstex )

Lawmakers backing an Illinois bill that would clear the way for balcony solar have ended their push to pass the measure this year. With three weeks still to go in the legislative session, they hit a stalemate as opponents, including the state's powerful electrical workers union, raised concerns about safety.

Advocates and lawmakers who had championed the bill were unhappy with an amendment filed on April 24 that would have prohibited the small plug-in solar panels until national standards are updated to include them.

Supporters say that other states have passed balcony solar laws without that provision. They contend that plugging in a small solar panel is no more dangerous than plugging in a washing machine or hair dryer.

'The whole point of the bill is to make these things safe,' said Kady McFadden, an Illinois lobbyist who focuses on clean energy and was leading legislative strategy for Senate Bill 3104. 'It's finding the right pieces to make sure consumers are safe, and also balancing that with being able to deploy these things.'

Given the amendment and concerns from stakeholders including the union, the lawmakers sponsoring the bill decided against further action, according to McFadden.

Environmental and clean energy groups have worked closely with lawmakers in Illinois and around the country to promote plug-in solar as a way to help people lower their energy bills and access small-scale renewable energy technology. Utah passed the country's first balcony solar bill in 2025, and Maine and Colorado followed suit this spring. More than half the states nationwide are considering balcony solar bills, and legislators have sent them to governors' desks in Maryland and Virginia.

The Illinois bill specified that interconnection agreements from utilities would not be necessary for plug-in solar arrays of up to 1,200 watts, and that landlords and homeowners' associations could not enact barriers to arrays of 391 watts or smaller. Balcony solar systems worldwide typically range from 200 watts to 1,600 watts.

An amendment filed in February would have prohibited any plug-in solar systems greater than 391 watts until the National Electrical Code, a widely used set of safety standards, is updated to include these arrays - a move that is not scheduled until late 2028. The April amendment would have barred any plug-in solar at all until those standards are amended.

'The wait until that code update could be years in the future,' said Kavi Chintam, Illinois campaign manager for the advocacy group Vote Solar. Chintam noted that the bill would have required plug-in solar arrays to be certified by UL Solutions (formerly Underwriters Laboratories), which sets safety standards for appliances and other household goods, or an equivalent organization.

In the state Senate, 139 residents or stakeholder groups filed ' witness slips' in support of the balcony solar bill, while three filed in opposition, including the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers state conference. The representative who filed the IBEW's slip did not respond to requests for comment for this story, nor did two IBEW local chapters.

'We were disappointed to see the opposition' from electricians, said McFadden, noting that a balcony solar bill recently unanimously passed the state Senate in New York, 'which also is a labor-heavy state.'

Bill supporters believed they wouldn't be able to reach consensus on a measure that would allow Illinoisans to quickly adopt balcony solar, so they decided to wait for a future legislative session, McFadden said.

IBEW representatives also raised concerns about the safety of plug-in solar in Oregon, where a bill this spring failed to pass before the session ended.

McFadden said that during the legislative debate, the union representatives didn't bring up fears about how the arrays might impact workers' jobs, but proponents think IBEW may be concerned that plug-in solar does not require an electrician for installation. Chintam said advocates hope to reassure union members that balcony solar would not cut into demand for rooftop solar, which creates jobs for installers and electricians.

'This is a completely different market than what rooftop solar does,' Chintam said. 'It would be small potatoes in terms of the kilowatts that rooftop solar brings. Obviously, we care about the workforce part of solar, but this is a totally different category meant to be purely for appliance-level savings.'

McFadden noted that in the past, it's taken more than one legislative session to enact clean energy bills, most recently the 2025 Clean and Reliable Grid Act as well as sweeping bills that passed in 2021 and 2016.

'Illinois is unique - we like to do things our own way in the legislative process,' said McFadden. 'It takes more than a year to pass a bill.'

Link to the original story.

value.Title: April 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Appoints Carly Reiner Caceres as National Campus Recruiting HR Director

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGN-XMH3-RTF8-S186-00000-00April 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Appoints Carly Reiner Caceres as National Campus Recruiting HR Director2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:45ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 27, 2026: Clark Construction Group Appoints Carly Reiner Caceres as National Campus Recruiting HR Director

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.

Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.

Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.

Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.

Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

SECTION 1 CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-K913-SCG7-44J8-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGJ-RGS3-RS9T-F3BV-00000-00April 27, 2026: Carly Reiner Caceres Named HR Director for Clark Construction Group2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:45ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 27, 2026: Carly Reiner Caceres Named HR Director for Clark Construction Group

Carly Reiner Caceres has been named Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, with the appointment taking effect on Apr. 29, 2026. In this capacity, she will lead Clark Construction Group's national campus recruiting initiatives to strengthen early-career talent pipelines for one of the nation’s largest building and infrastructure companies.

Prior to joining Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she helped shape HR strategy and operations in a hybrid work environment. Her career also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she worked as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead, along with campus recruiting leadership at Grant Thornton LLP.

Caceres’ background in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding aligns with Clark Construction Group’s agenda to attract top university talent nationwide. Her early career included internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, underscoring a broad communications and external-relations base that informs her approach to talent development.

Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

urn:contentItem:6JGG-F693-RS9D-Y4NS-00000-00CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:40:40ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC

PermID4296619307Websitehttp://www.clarkconstruction.comIndustryConstructionAddress18201 Von Karman Avenue Suite 800 Irvine, California 92612

ACTIVITIES:

Clark Construction Group, LLC, founded in 1906, is a leading general contractor headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland. With over 4,000 employees across 20+ offices, it serves commercial, infrastructure, and institutional sectors, delivering projects like stadiums, hospitals, and transit systems. Generating over $5 billion annually, Clark is known for its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices. In 2024, it completed major infrastructure projects, emphasizing green construction. Its culture prioritizes safety, diversity, and employee development, earning industry accolades. Competing with Turner Construction, Clark stands out for its large-scale project expertise and client partnerships. Recent initiatives include expanding renewable energy construction and digital tools for project management.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

+ VPM AND CLARK CONSTRUCTION OPEN NEW HEADQUARTERS TO NEXT-GENERATION BUILDERS

VPM hosted students from the Metropolitan Business League at its new headquarters, which is under construction by Clark Construction. The event, part of a 'Youth in Construction' initiative, occurred on 19th November 2025 and gave Richmond Technical Center students an inside view of trades engaged in major projects, including carpentry, electrical work, HVAC, and plumbing. The session also featured Baker Concrete's apprenticeship program, illustrating the numerous career options within the industry. This immersive experience allowed students to imagine themselves contributing to similar developments in the future. VPM began work on its new headquarters in August 2024, with completion slated for 2026. The Monroe Ward relocation is intended to centralize VPM’s news and content production, increasing accessibility for the public. By expanding its presence in the Richmond area, VPM aims to strengthen coverage of local news and events. In addition to trade demonstrations, students had the chance to engage with VPM's broadcasting capabilities, recording clips in the mobile sound booth to gain firsthand understanding of how the organization delivers news to millions through TV and radio channels. Community support remains a key element for VPM, enabling ongoing local news coverage, educational programming, and inspiring content. As VPM progresses its development with Clark Construction, the collaboration exemplifies how community-oriented initiatives can enrich local education and industry awareness.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD CAROLINA AVIATION OPERATIONS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026, tasking him with leading and expanding the firm's aviation practice across North and South Carolina. With 19 years in construction and more than 12 years at Balfour Beatty, Johnson has delivered 22 aviation projects, including work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport. In his new role, he will support existing aviation clients and broaden the regional footprint at airports throughout the Carolinas, while emphasising safety, continuity, and effective coordination with stakeholders. He earned a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and participates in community initiatives such as the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK NAMED TO BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK, EXPANDING RAIL GEOTECHNICAL CAPABILITIES

Mainmark UK has joined the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, enabling it to support Network Rail’s CP7 programme from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an estimated £43–45 billion allocated across Great Britain.CP7 focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway, with priorities including maintenance, renewals, digital signaling, safety improvements, and enhanced freight and passenger performance.Mainmark UK will contribute through geotechnical response work, bridge repairs, structural projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks, under low complexity calls, preventative maintenance, and small civil schemes.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager of Mainmark UK, said, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans." Mainmark’s Teretek resin injection technology stabilises weak ground and fills voids without heavy excavation, helping maintain rail integrity as freight loads and weather events grow. The CP7 appointment signals a continued role for Mainmark in enhancing resilience and reliability of the UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY’S DUAL-MARKET STRATEGY DELIVERS PROFITABILITY AMID RISING COSTS

Balfour Beatty PLC reported solid growth, driven by its construction division as power-transmission work and US building projects supported the top line. The group’s market value rose roughly two-thirds over the last year as it benefits from a record order book, while underlying earnings per share increased 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the company said performance was aided by UK power-transmission demand and a pickup in US activity. Its support services unit lifted operating profit by a third to £122m, with the underlying margin improving by 80 basis points to 8.5%, and the UK construction arm hitting margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, higher project volumes helped offset cost overruns on a Civils project, underscoring the benefits of Balfour Beatty’s dual-market approach. Analysts remain optimistic about its outlook as the order book grows and infrastructure investment continues.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SHAPES RESILIENCE-DRIVEN URBAN RENEWAL IN SOUTH FLORIDA

On the 28th of March 2026, an industry roundtable hosted with the South Florida Business Journal gathered leaders from construction, development, and public-private sectors to discuss the rapid skyline transformation unfolding across the region and DPR Construction’s role in it.Collaboration and integrated project delivery were highlighted as essential in South Florida’s crowded, high stakes market. By aligning stakeholders early, teams can achieve faster schedules, safer sites, and higher quality, while navigating limited land and rising costs through more thoughtful, resilient design.Innovation and technology were identified as key accelerants. DPR Construction and partners are advancing prefabrication, modular approaches, and data driven planning to shorten timelines and improve accuracy. The emphasis on digital tools supports better forecasting and coordination across multi phase efforts, with safety and sustainability guiding decision making.With growth comes the need for a skilled workforce, educational partnerships, and robust training pipelines to sustain momentum. The conversation also underscored the importance of building climate resilient infrastructure and community centered design to ensure new developments endure.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER REACHES STRUCTURAL MILESTONE AS TURNER MOVES TOWARD FIT-OUT

Turner Construction Company celebrated a major milestone at the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, with the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility on the 28th of April 2026. The project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours and involves about 345 skilled workers. The center spans 450,000 square feet on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel frame, an insulated precast exterior, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling system that blends liquid and air-cooled technologies. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," commented Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." With the structure now in place, attention turns to interior fit-out and readiness as the project progresses toward activation and continued support for the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER LEADS 11-STORY GEISINGER TOWER BUILD IN $900M VALLEY UPGRADE

Turner Construction has kicked off vertical construction on the $900 million Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center expansion in Wilkes-Barre, a milestone in one of the region's largest health care investments. Steel is rising on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot tower that will increase beds from 356 to 500 and add operating rooms from 12 to 18, while enlarging the emergency department and ensuring all private rooms. The project targets a first-quarter 2028 opening. This announcement was issued on 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, said the project represents expanded access and a modern health care platform for families across northeastern Pennsylvania, noting the company’s pride in partnering with Geisinger. The expansion is expected to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Peak onsite staffing could reach 450 workers, with Geisinger planning to hire 400 more after completion.Originally opened in 1981, the Wyoming Valley campus has grown to meet demand, and the new tower will bring capabilities on par with major academic medical centers serving large cities.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION TO SPEARHEAD WESTCOURT ORLANDO CONSTRUCTION IN MAJOR DOWNTOWN MIXED-USE SCHEME

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

urn:contentItem:6JGB-F2B3-S5GJ-81VG-00000-00ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisSTOCKROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION

PermID4296528943Websitehttps://www.rockefellerfoundation.org/IndustryPhilanthropy / Global DevelopmentAddress420 Fifth Avenue, New York City, New York, U.S.

ACTIVITIES:

The Rockefeller Foundation, founded in 1913 by John D. Rockefeller, is a global philanthropy headquartered in New York City. With around 200 staff, it manages a $6 billion endowment, granting $300 million annually to advance health, equity, and sustainability. In 2024, it committed $100 million to climate resilience in African cities. Its culture emphasizes innovation, collaboration, and data-driven impact, with training in global policy and grantmaking. Competing with Ford Foundation, Rockefeller stands out for its pioneering role in public health and green energy, including founding the Global Energy Alliance for People and Planet. Recent initiatives include AI for social good and support for the National Museum of African American History, aligning with social justice trends. The foundations century-long legacy includes eradicating hookworm and funding the Green Revolution, shaping modern philanthropy.

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES SECOND CLASS OF U.S. BIG BETS FELLOWS AT BALTIMORE CONVENING

10 bold innovators from across the United States named to The Rockefeller Foundation fellowship dedicated to driving economic opportunity and helping their communities thrive.

Fellows focusing on distinct projects to help their communities realize economic opportunities, including: expanding workforce pathways, deploying blended capital for climate resilience, building AI-powered talent-matching tools, scaling registered apprenticeships, and strengthening regional innovation ecosystems.

Source: Company Website

+ ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION ACCELERATES U.S. ECONOMIC SOLUTIONS AT "BIG BETS FOR AMERICA: BALTIMORE"

BALTIMORE | April 21, 2026 - The Rockefeller Foundation today hosted Big Bets for America: Baltimore, bringing together more than 250 leaders spanning policy, philanthropic, private, and non-profit sectors in Baltimore to surface, accelerate, and scale ambitious solutions to the country's most pressing challenges. With a Steering Committee - consisting of Abell Foundation, Baltimore Community Foundation, Baltimore Homecoming, Greater Baltimore Committee, and The Harry and Jeanette Weinberg Foundation - and strategic partners Greater Washington Partnership, Baltimore Development Corporation, and Johns Hopkins University, the Foundation and event participants, including Maryland Governor Wes Moore, Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott, and Cleveland Mayor Justin Bibb, announced new initiatives and innovative collaborations to create economic pathways and advance opportunities for communities across the State of Maryland and nationwide. In Baltimore, The Rockefeller Foundation also launched a $100 million commitment to connect America's workers to good jobs and its next class of U.S. Big Bets Fellows.

Source: Company Website

+ STATEMENT FROM DR. RAJIV J. SHAH, PRESIDENT OF THE ROCKEFELLER FOUNDATION, ON CHANGE TO U.S. AID APPROACH

For decades, American global leadership has been built on a foundational, bipartisan belief that helping those who need it most is also an investment in our own security and prosperity. A shift away from development assistance to focus solely on trade raises serious concerns about the implications for the world's most vulnerable people and for long‑term U.S. interests.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ FORD FOUNDATION [3 Press Releases]

+ FORD FOUNDATION PROMOTE THE VOTE FUND: ENSURING EVERY VOICE IS HEARD IN MICHIGAN

In Michigan, local leaders work tirelessly to ensure that voting is accessible, elections are free and fair, and every eligible voter knows their rights. This strong civic participation doesn't happen overnight: It's the result of year-round, coordinated work by organizations to help people understand their rights, navigate the voting process, and come together in their communities to engage and collaborate on meaningful changes.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION THE COST OF I DO IN THE DISABILITY COMMUNITY

When Patrice Jetter and Garry Wickham became engaged, the first decisions were of small, giddy consequence: where to hold the ceremony, who to invite, what to wear. But soon enough, a far weightier question eclipsed them all: whether they could afford to marry at all. They learned that, as people with disabilities who receive Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Medicaid coverage from the American government, their benefits will likely be cut if they wed, or even moved in together.

Source: Company Website

+ FORD FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES 2026 COHORT OF FORD GLOBAL FELLOWSHIP

NEW YORK - The Ford Foundation today announced the 2026 cohort of the Ford Global Fellowship, welcoming 32 leaders into a burgeoning network that now exceeds 150 fellows working across Africa, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East. This year's cohort reinforces Ford's 90-year commitment to strengthening democratic values by investing in those closest to the world's most persistent inequalities.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: Ameresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JV

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJD-BM73-S7PH-455W-00000-00Ameresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JV2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:47ZLexisNexisAmeresco Spins Off Biofuels Business Into HASI JVby Mihaela Miteva

Ameresco Inc. (AMRC) and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc. (HASI) on Monday, May 4, said they've agreed to spin off Ameresco's biofuels business into a new joint venture, Neogenyx Fuels.The deal values Neogenyx Fuels at a post-money enterprise value of $1.8 billion.

Ameresco will own 70% of the venture, while HASI will own the remaining 30%.HASI has committed $400 million to the transaction. Of that amount, $300 million will be invested directly in Neogenyx Fuels to support growth, while $100 million will be paid to Ameresco for the existing business. Framingham, Mass.-based Ameresco said it will use the proceeds for strategic opportunities, working capital and deleveraging.Neogenyx Fuels will develop, own and operate advanced fuel assets aimed at supporting the energy transition. Michael T. Bakas, president of renewable fuels at Ameresco, will be CEO of the new company.HASI president and CEO Jeffrey A. Lipson said in the statement the transaction builds on the company's relationship with Ameresco, including more than 60 joint transactions since 2001.Ameresco provides energy infrastructure and efficiency services, upgrades aging infrastructure and develops distributed energy resources for federal, state and local governments, utilities, data centers, schools, healthcare institutions, housing authorities and commercial and industrial customers. In 2023, it picked up Italian renewable energy and energy efficiency company Enerqos Energy Solutions Srl.Annapolis, Md.-based HASI, previously called Hannon Armstrong Sustainable Infrastructure Capital Inc., invests in sustainable infrastructure assets, including utility-scale solar, storage, onshore wind, distributed solar and storage, renewable natural gas and energy efficiency. In May 2024, HASI and KKR & Co. (KKR) formed CarbonCount Holdings 1 LLC, with a joint commitment to invest up to $2 billion in climate-positive projects across the U.S.The companies expect to close the Neogenyx Fuels transaction this quarter.Ameresco turned to Guggenheim Securities LLC for financial advice and to Cyril V. Jones, J. Heath Mayo and David B. Feirstein of Kirkland & Ellis LLP for counsel.Gregory Hort and Sergio de la Torre of Lazard Ltd. provided financial advice to HASI, which looked to Daniel S. Alterbaum, Nicholas H. Politan Jr. and Jonathan R. Sapp of Gibson, Dunn &Crutcher LLP for counsel.

DEAL SIZE

$ 1-10 Billion
